evidence_scope: correctness and routing-mechanism verification, NOT a cost/performance A/B benchmark
note: Unlike the Codex mirror's model-routing-benchmark-example.json (which A/B-compares token/time cost between two routing strategies on repeated matched workloads), this file has no comparative cost baseline to A/B against -- Claude Code has no equivalent dual-arm benchmark harness wired up yet. What follows is real pass/fail evidence: each coding task was independently, objectively graded by a deterministic script, not by model self-report or my own subjective read.

algorithm_fidelity_test.description: 24-assertion automated test suite (comprehensive_test.py) run against auto_model.py from a clean-slate ledger, covering every branch of the boundary-search algorithm ported from Codex's obsidian_model_memory.py.

real_graded_coding_tasks.grading_method: deterministic script checked against a brute-force-independently-verified reference table
real_graded_coding_tasks.caveat: First grading run reported FAIL; before trusting that, the reference table was independently re-verified by brute force and found to contain a wrong hand-typed value (my error, not the model's). Corrected and re-graded.

real_graded_coding_tasks.grading_method: deterministic script, 6 hand-reasoned cases including touching-interval merge rule and unsorted input

real_graded_coding_tasks.task: evaluate_expression(expr) -- arithmetic with precedence, unary minus, parentheses, no eval()
real_graded_coding_tasks.grading_method: deterministic script, 8 hand-computed cases; source additionally inspected to confirm a real recursive-descent parser (no eval()/exec() shortcut)

note_on_boundary_search_evidence: All three real graded tasks passed on the first attempt at the cheap-first cold-start pair (haiku|low). No genuine quality failure occurred in this session to organically trigger the upgrade/downgrade boundary search on real data -- that path is proven correct via the algorithm_fidelity_test above (scripted pass/fail inputs) but has not yet been exercised by a real failure. Deliberately not fabricated here.

known_untested_gap: Genuine operational failure (a real Agent call actually erroring/crashing) has not occurred and cannot be forced on demand honestly -- that path is verified only via scripted ledger entries, disclosed as such.
